commented_code:
+name: Lint PR
+
+on:
+  pull_request_target:
+    types:
+      - opened
+      - reopened
+      - edited
+
+jobs:
+  main:
+    name: Validate PR title
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    permissions:
+      pull-requests: read
+    steps:
+      - uses: amannn/action-semantic-pull-request@v6
+        env:
+          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

discussion_comments.commented_code:

discussion_comments.comment_body: Wait, what does that do? Why is a token needed for this check?

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+name: Lint PR
+
+on:
+  pull_request_target:
+    types:
+      - opened
+      - reopened
+      - edited
+
+jobs:
+  main:
+    name: Validate PR title
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    permissions:
+      pull-requests: read
+    steps:
+      - uses: amannn/action-semantic-pull-request@v6
+        env:
+          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
discussion_comments.comment_body:
It needs it to fetch the PR + commits from the github REST API, it's pretty standard. 

It's [the first example on the repo README](https://github.com/amannn/action-semantic-pull-request), `GITHUB_TOKEN` is automatically generated for workflows.

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+name: Lint PR
+
+on:
+  pull_request_target:
+    types:
+      - opened
+      - reopened
+      - edited
+
+jobs:
+  main:
+    name: Validate PR title
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    permissions:
+      pull-requests: read
+    steps:
+      - uses: amannn/action-semantic-pull-request@v6
+        env:
+          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
discussion_comments.comment_body: Ok, I wasn't familiar with this part of the GitHub Actions system. I went down a quick rabbit hole and this should indeed be fine even if the action were malicious. Thanks!
